title: Law Enforcement Complaints * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Law Enforcement Complaints * Native American Community 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - LGBTQ2+ Individuals Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CALL BLACKLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a hotline focusing on Black, Indigenous, People of Color, and LGBTQ2+ individuals who have experienced harmful, physical, and inappropriate contact with police and vigilantes. Provides an anonymous and confidential avenue to report interactions and referrals to resources. Volunteers are trained to listen and affirm the experiences of anyone who may have experienced a negative interaction with law enforcement, consumer service (restaurants and stores), or vigilante contact. Volunteer opportunities are available. Confidential Mailing Address Eligibility: Unrestricted; prioritizes BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, and People of Color) and LGBTQ2+ individuals Hours: Monday-Friday 6am-2pm, Saturday-Sunday 4pm-8pm Email (800) 604-5841 Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
